creator: ahze@ahze.net
text:
# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#       /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/
#       /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/Makefile
#       /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/distinfo
#       /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-comment
#       /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-descr
#       /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-plist
#       /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/files
#       /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/files/patch-aa
#
echo c - /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/
mkdir -p /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/ > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/Makefile << 'END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/Makefile'
X# Ports collection makefile for:       OpenVerse
X# Date created:                24 Feb 2001
X# Whom:                        Michael Johnson <ahze@ahze.net>
X#
X
XPORTNAME=      OpenVerse
XPORTVERSION=   0.8-3
XCATEGORIES=    net tk82
XMASTER_SITES=  http://www.openverse.org/files/OpenVerse/
XDISTFILES=     OpenVerse.tar.gz
XMAINTAINER=    ahze@ahze.net
X
X
XRUN_DEPENDS=    tclsh8.2:${PORTSDIR}/lang/tcl82 \
X                wish8.2:${PORTSDIR}/x11-toolkits/tk82
X
XNO_BUILD=      yes
XWRKSRC=         ${WRKDIR}/OpenVerse
Xdo-install:$
X       ${MKDIR} ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/       \
X                ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/anims  \
X                ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/icons  \
X                ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/images \
X                ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/language \
X                ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/lib    \
X                ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/panels \
X                ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/plugins \
X                ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/rooms  \
X                ${PREFIX}/share/doc/OpenVerse/ \
X                ${PREFIX}/share/doc/OpenVerse/help
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/anims/* ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/anims
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/icons/* ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/icons
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/images/* ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/images
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/language/* ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/language
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/lib/* ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/lib
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/panels/* ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/panels
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/plugins/* ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/plugins
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/rooms/* ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/rooms
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/OpenVerse.tcl ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/OpenVerse.tcl
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/server.cfg ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/server.cfg
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/server.tcl ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/server.tcl
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/passageways.txt ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/passageways.txt
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/help/* ${PREFIX}/share/doc/OpenVerse/help
X       ${CP} -R ${WRKSRC}/README ${PREFIX}/share/doc/OpenVerse/README
X
Xpost-install:
X       @${ECHO} "Please read the documentation for OpenVerse located in"
X       @${ECHO} "${PREFIX}/share/doc/OpenVerse/help and ${PREFIX}/share/doc/OpenVerse/README."
X       ${LN} -f ${PREFIX}/lib/OpenVerse/OpenVerse.tcl ${PREFIX}/bin/OpenVerse
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/Makefile
echo x - /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/distinfo << 'END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/distinfo'
XMD5 (OpenVerse.tar.gz) = bc599bcf8dac44cac9dc6a33557eee7b
END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/distinfo
echo x - /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-comment
sed 's/^X//' >/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-comment << 'END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-comment'
XOpenVerse is a visual chat program written in Tcl/Tk
END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-comment
echo x - /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-descr << 'END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-descr'
XFrom Web Page:
X       OpenVerse is a visual chat program written in Tcl/Tk. The program connects to
X        any OpenVerse server (server and client code included).
X
X       The OpenVerse is similar to other graphical chat systems like The Palace and
X        Microsoft Chat with the main difference being that it is much more OPEN and also runs
X        on a variety of systems including Windows, *nix (Yes Linux too), Macintosh OS/2 you
X        name it! Anything which Tcl/Tk is available for should run OpenVerse just fine!
X
XWWW: http://openverse.org/END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-descr
echo x - /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-plist << 'END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/pkg-plist'

echo c - /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/files
mkdir -p /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - /usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/files/patch-aa
sed 's/^X//' >/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/files/patch-aa << 'END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/files/patch-aa'
X--- OpenVerse.tcl.orig Sat Feb 24 02:52:45 2001
X+++ OpenVerse.tcl      Sat Feb 24 02:53:58 2001
X@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
X-#!/usr/bin/wish
X+#!/usr/local/bin/wish8.2
X #
X # OpenVerse Main Module 
X # 
X@@ -91,7 +91,7 @@
X               # check environment variable OVRUNDIR
X               if {[info exists env(OVRUNDIR)] == 0} {
X                       # not set, use standard
X-                      set OvRunDir /usr/lib/OpenVerse
X+                      set OvRunDir /usr/local/lib/OpenVerse
X               } else {
X                       # it's set, run from there
X                       set OvRunDir $env(OVRUNDIR)
END-of-/usr/ports/net/OpenVerse/files/patch-aa
exit
